summary: A single fourslash test failed with an AssertionError: the code fix produced 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' but the test expected 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…\'' (a more complete generic type). This caused the test suite to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The fourslash test 'codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts' failed because the code fix for missing type annotations produced an oversimplified return type 'Foo<string>' instead of the expected fuller generic type 'Foo<string, string[…'.
relevant_tests: fourslash tests > tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts > fourslash test codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts runs correctly

evidence.quote: AssertionError: expected 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' to equal 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…'
evidence.reason: This is the exact assertion mismatch: the code fix produced a simplified generic type 'Foo<string>' but the test expected a more complete generic type 'Foo<string, string[…'.
Confirms exactly one test failed out of 106366 passing tests.
Identifies the code path where the assertion failed — in the verifyCodeFix method, confirming this is a code fix behavior mismatch.
Confirms the test runner exited with a non-zero code due to the test failure.

suggested_fix: Fix the 'codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts' test or the underlying code fix logic so that the generated return type annotation for generic types includes all type parameters (e.g., 'Foo<string, string[…>' instead of the oversimplified 'Foo<string>').
